name: Shoulder Osteochondrosis
description: Shoulder osteochondrosis (OC) involves abnormal cartilage maturation at the humeral head, forming a cartilage flap that causes pain and joint inflammation. It is the most common site for OCD in dogs and affects young large-breed dogs.
symptoms: Pain on shoulder extension and flexion
treatment_overview: Arthroscopic removal of the cartilage flap is the preferred treatment with excellent outcomes. Medical management with rest and NSAIDs may help mild cases but is rarely definitive.
prevention_tips: Avoid excessive calcium supplementation in puppies
Controlled exercise in young large-breed dogs
